turns.content: I'm trying to add build metadata to my crate but I have no idea why the output is so messy. It seems like I need to manually parse environment variables and then somehow format them into a version string that ends up in the binary, yet when I run cargo build it just spits out raw env values without any human-readable formatting.

The confusion usually comes from mixing how metadata is embedded versus how it's exposed at runtime. You want to stamp the git SHA and timestamp into the binary using build.rs, but then use env! macro at runtime to read those values back out so you can print them with a proper version string.
So I should be writing something like env!(const BUILD_SHA = "..."), right? And that will automatically add the metadata to the binary without any extra steps?

Not quite, because const env! doesn't work as a direct replacement for your manual string formatting logic. You need to use the env! macro in build.rs to extract values from environment variables, then construct a String or Vec<String> manually before calling the metadata stamping function so you can control how it appears.
Ah okay, I see. So I need to actually build the string myself in build.rs and pass that to the stamping macro instead of relying on const env! directly?

Exactly, you construct the metadata string by calling env! multiple times for each variable like git sha, timestamp, and version, then concatenate them into a single String before invoking the stamping macro. This allows you to format it however you want in build.rs so that when cargo builds your crate, the human-readable --version output reflects your custom concatenation of those values.
